dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3587 IS: No Tax on Wrongful Delay Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Blackburn introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide an exemption from gross income for interest paid to taxpayers by the Internal Revenue Service following an audit or litigation in which the taxpayer prevailed.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Tax on Wrongful Delay Act of 2026.

section.header: Exemption from gross income for interest paid to taxpayers following audit or litigation

subsection.text: Part III of subchapter B of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting before section 140 the following new section:

section.header: Interest paid to taxpayers following an audit or litigation
section.text: Gross income shall not include any interest which, pursuant to section 6611, is required to be paid upon any overpayment in respect of any internal revenue tax following—

paragraph.text: an examination pursuant to the provisions of section 7602,
any suit or proceeding brought by the taxpayer for the credit or refund of taxes, or
any civil action commenced by the United States for the collection or recovery of taxes.

subsection.text: The table of sections for part III of subchapter B of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting before the item relating to section 140 the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 139M. Interest paid to taxpayers following an audit or litigation.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.
